Aggression: An agent directs hostile action against a target (goad, abuse, bully, tease). The grammatical subject is the aggressor (animate or institutional).
Buying: Agent acquires goods/services from a source in exchange for payment  (buy, book, order, purchase). The grammatical subject is the buyer.
Capacity: A container or space has the capacity to hold/house/seat a specified quantity or entity (seat, sleep, hold, store). The grammatical subject is the container/space.
Carrying: Agent co-moves a theme through space  (carry, take, bring, haul). The grammatical subject is the carrier (agent or vehicle, co-moving with theme).
Cause_Change_of_State: Agent causes a theme to undergo a named state change  (cut, bake, dice, slice). The grammatical subject is the causer/agent.
Cause_Creation: Agent causes a new entity to come into existence  (make, play, cut, construct). The grammatical subject is the creator/maker.
Cause_Incremental: Agent applies/removes/places material along a surface or into a container, with a scale of coverage, location, or embedment (bury, put, siphon). The grammatical subject is the agent.
Cause_Membership: Agent causes a person/entity to enter or leave a group/role  (hire, recall, remove, expel). The grammatical subject is the authority/organization.
Cause_Participation: Agent designates/elects/nominates a person to a specific role or disqualifies them  (elect, appoint, nominate, designate). The grammatical subject is the electorate/authority.
Cause_and_Effect: Event/entity/action brings about an outcome  (cause, provoke, raise, generate). The grammatical subject is the cause (often an event nominal or abstract entity).
Change_of_State: A theme undergoes a state change without an overt causer  (grow, darken, change, bake). The grammatical subject is the theme/patient (the entity that changes).
Charge: Agent imposes a financial/material debit on a recipient OR the inverse  (charge, bill). The grammatical subject is the agent imposing cost, or transaction itself (cost as subject).
Collective: Agent join or participate in a group/activity (join, participate). The grammatical subject is the joiner/participant.
Communicate: Agent transmits information to a recipient  (tell, ask, warn, inquire). The grammatical subject is the speaker/sender.
Concealment: Agent or element hides, blocks, or masks something from view/access (hide, block, sequester, screen). The grammatical subject is the concealer (agent or inanimate obstructor).
Constrain: Agent wears or dons an item of clothing/accessory; rare: contains (wear, hold). The grammatical subject is the wearer.
Contact: Agent makes physical contact with a theme, typically with short, bounded force  (punch, touch, gouge, beat). The grammatical subject is the contactor/agent.
Counting: Agent enumerates or tallies items in a collection (count, add). The grammatical subject is the counter.
Desire: Experiencer has an attitude toward a wanted/preferred outcome  (need, want, desire). The grammatical subject is the experiencer.
Discovery: Experiencer comes to know something — by finding, learning , guessing, or ascertaining (learn, guess, find, read). The grammatical subject is the discoverer/knower.
Dynamic_Possession: Agent gains, transfers, or holds a theme transiently; non-commercial change of possession (borrow, receive, donate, collect). The grammatical subject is the recipient/holder (or sometimes source).
Emission: Source emits light, sound, smell, or substance  (flicker, shine, bleed, reek). The grammatical subject is the emitter.
Emotion_(exp. subj.): Experiencer has an emotion directed at/concerning a theme  (like, support, enjoy, fear). The grammatical subject is the experiencer.
Emotion_(stim. subj.): Stimulus causes an experiencer to feel an emotion  (worry, bother, concern, rag). The grammatical subject is the stimulus.
Enforcement: Agent/institution exerts authority to maintain rules, regulations, or conditions  (control, enforce). The grammatical subject is the enforcer/authority.
Evaluation: Agent assesses the value, quality, or nature of a theme  (estimate, consider, evaluate, analyze). The grammatical subject is the evaluator.
Existence: An entity exists, persists, or is located/present  (be, wait, remain, happen). The grammatical subject is the existent.
Feeding: Agent provides food/nourishment to a consumer  (feed, bottle-feed, nurse, wean). The grammatical subject is the feeder.
Form: Agent or natural process brings a structure/organism into shape or arrangement  (grow, cultivate, farm, rear). The grammatical subject is the shaper or forming-entity (natural or agentive).
Future_Having: Agent provides a future claim or entitlement to a recipient  (issue, offer, owe, guarantee). The grammatical subject is the grantor/debtor.
Hurt: Agent or event causes physical/emotional harm to an entity  (hurt, cut, burn, scald). The grammatical subject is the harmer (agent or impersonal source).
Image_Creation: Agent produces a durable representation/record  (record, print, copy, tape). The grammatical subject is the recorder.
Incremental: Substance/material fills, covers, or distributes across a surface or along a path — often uncaused/stative (hang, plaster, spray, seal). The grammatical subject is the theme (the distributed substance) or a ground that is covered.
Induction: Agent influences another agent's behavior through argument, pressure, or manipulation  (goad, persuade, compel, cheat). The grammatical subject is the inducer.
Ingestion: Agent consumes food or a substance  (eat, drink, graze, smoke). The grammatical subject is the consumer (self-directed).
Intention: Experiencer aims at, seeks, or pursues a goal cognitively  (hope, look, pray, search). The grammatical subject is the intender/seeker.
Joint_Statement: Two or more parties reciprocally communicate or reach alignment  (agree, debate, cooperate, disagree). The grammatical subject is the co-participants.
Learning: Agent engages in study or acquisition of knowledge/skill  (learn, read, study, memorize). The grammatical subject is the learner.
Limitation: Agent or factor restricts the extent, quantity, or permissibility of something  (limit, restrict, reduce, restrain). The grammatical subject is the limiter (agent or inanimate restriction).
Location: Entity is located at, stays at, or emerges into a place  (adjoin, come, appear, stop). The grammatical subject is the located-entity / path-entity.
Manipulation: Agent uses or operates an instrument/tool/object  (use, work, drive). The grammatical subject is the user/operator.
Measurement: Agent or object registers/reports a quantitative property  (cost, measure, take, total). The grammatical subject is the measured-entity (as subject of `cost/measure/weigh`) or measurer.
Membership: Agent is a member of, manages, or belongs to a group or institution  (attend, manage, oversee, belong). The grammatical subject is the member or supervisor.
Motion: Entity undergoes self-directed displacement or a theme follows a path  (come, leave, cut, travel). The grammatical subject is the mover.
Participation: Agent fills or enacts a role  (serve, act, host, behave). The grammatical subject is the role-filler.
Payment: Agent pays  (pay). The grammatical subject is the payer.
Perception_(exp._subj.): Experiencer perceives a stimulus  (see, look, hear, notice). The grammatical subject is the experiencer.
Perception_(stim._subj.): Stimulus appears/sounds/feels a certain way to an implicit experiencer  (look, taste, sound, feel). The grammatical subject is the stimulus.
Pick_Up_and_Drop_Off: Agent moves a theme to a specified endpoint  (drop, lift, raise, lower). The grammatical subject is the agent.
Possession: Possessor owns/has a theme statively (own, belong, have). The grammatical subject is the possessor.
Protection: Agent shields a target from harm  (protect, defend, guard, safeguard). The grammatical subject is the protector.
Pursuit: Physical co-motion with adversarial or pursuit relation , OR directed search activity toward a target . The common thread is directed motion or effort toward a target that has not yet been reached (chase, lead, steer, misdirect). The grammatical subject is the pursuer/guide.
Reciprocal: Two or more parties engage symmetrically  (meet, visit, fight, marry). The grammatical subject is the co-participants.
Replacement: One entity replaces/succeeds/exchanges/ousts another  (replace, substitute, switch, swap). The grammatical subject is the successor / replacement-agent.
Request: Agent requests/demands/commands an action from a target  (ask, urge, order, demand). The grammatical subject is the requester/commander.
Response: Agent reacts to a prior act, statement, or threat  (face, respond, answer, confront). The grammatical subject is the responder.
Selling: Seller transfers goods to a buyer in exchange for money (sell, hawk). The grammatical subject is the seller.
Sending: Agent dispatches a theme to a destination without accompanying  (send, transport, export, import). The grammatical subject is the sender.
Statement: Agent asserts or reports a proposition  (say, talk, comment, declare). The grammatical subject is the speaker.
Throwing: Agent propels a theme through the air via impulse  (fire, launch, throw, toss). The grammatical subject is the thrower/propeller.
Transfer_of_Possession: Agent takes possession involuntarily or coercively from a source  (steal, rob, extort, confiscate). The grammatical subject is the taker (or compensator in the compensation sub-frame).
Vehicular_Motion: Agent or vehicle traverses space using a vehicle  (ferry, paddle, ride, sail). The grammatical subject is the vehicle-operator or vehicle itself.
Abide_By: Agent acts upon Theme (abide_by, follow, heed, keep_to, adhere_to). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He followed the tenets of Buddhism.".
Accept: Agent acts upon Theme (accept, buy, understand, encourage). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I accepted how you did it.".
Acquiesce: Agent acts upon Pivot, Theme (defer, accede, bow, capitulate, fall). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The enemy soldiers yielded.".
Act: Agent acts upon Eventuality (action, carry_out, carry_through, conduct, execute). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They carried out the deed.".
Addict: Agent acts upon Patient, Theme (addict, bias, dispose, incline, predispose). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I addicted him to Douglas Adams.".
Adjust: Agent acts upon Patient, Goal, Source (adapt, accommodate, conform, adjust, gear). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He adapted himself.".
Admit: Agent acts upon Theme, Location (allow, let, hail, admit, include). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She admitted us.".
Adopt: Agent acts upon Theme (assume, adopt, take, take_on). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He assumed the position that dogs are people.".
Allow: Causer acts upon Beneficiary, Eventuality (approve, authorize, endorse, okay, sanction). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "They tolerated birds.".
Amalgamate: Agent acts upon Patient, Co-Patient (intermarry, couple, incorporate, integrate, match). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The folk songs alternated well with pop songs.".
Avoid: Agent acts upon Location, Theme (avoid, boycott, circumvent, dodge, duck). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "We avoided the area.".
Become: Patient acts upon Result (come_out, get, turn, turn_up, come). The grammatical subject is the patient. Example: "The belt came undone.".
Begin: Agent acts upon Eventuality, Subeventuality (go_on, proceed, set_out, set_about, begin). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He began to pack.".
Benefit: Causer acts upon Beneficiary (benefit, profit). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "Sunlight benefited plants.".
Berry: Agent acts upon Location (antique, berry, birdnest, blackberry, clam). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The children berried in the summer.".
Body_Motion: Agent acts upon Theme, Goal, Path (hold, extend, reach, stretch). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She reached her hand out.".
Calibratable_Cos: Patient acts upon Attribute, Extent, Initial_State (build, die, appreciate, climb, decline). The grammatical subject is the patient. Example: "The price of oil soared.".
Care: Experiencer acts upon Stimulus (mind, care, worry, wonder). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "He didn't care.".
Caring: Agent acts upon Theme (babysit, look_after, manage, mind, wait_on). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Jared looked after the children.".
Caused_Calibratable_Cos: Causer acts upon Patient, Extent, Initial_State (commute, cut, raise, reduce, advance). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "Lawmakers cut the budget by 10% from $1.46 million to $1.3 million.".
Complete: Agent acts upon Eventuality (accomplish, achieve, bring-off, complete, pull-off). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Wilma completed the assignment.".
Comprise: Theme acts upon Pivot (comprise, compose, make, make_up, constitute). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "Textiles make up the majority of exports.".
Conduct: Agent acts upon Theme, Beneficiary (conduct, give, head, hold, host). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The White House held a gala.".
Conspire: Agent acts upon Co-Agent, Theme, Maleficiary (ally, collaborate, collude, conspire, machinate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "John and Sarah conspired.".
Continue: Agent acts upon Eventuality, Subeventuality (continue). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He continued to pack.".
Convert: Agent acts upon Patient, Initial_State, Result (advert, come_around, fall, get, get_around). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He converted to believing in Buddha.".
Cope: Agent acts upon Theme (deal, cope, manage, grapple, get_by). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He managed with climbing the slope.".
Dedicate: Agent acts upon Theme, Goal (dedicate, devote, commit). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I dedicated myself to helping us.".
Delay: Agent acts upon Eventuality (postpone, delay, defer, stall, procrastinate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He delayed the watering.".
Discourage: Causer acts upon Eventuality, Beneficiary (dissuade, deter, discourage, hamper, hinder). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "The sign discouraged us from smoking.".
Disfunction: Theme engages in the named event (die, go_down, go_off, go_out, poop_out). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "My phone died.".
Empathize: Experiencer acts upon Stimulus (empathize, sympathize, identify, commiserate). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "I empathized.".
Enforce: Agent acts upon Theme (control, enforce, impose). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They enforced the rules.".
Establish: Agent acts upon Patient, Instrument (bring, constitute, constitutionalize, innovate, pioneer). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Zellig Harris established Penn's linguistics department.".
Exchange: Agent acts upon Theme, Co-Theme, Co-Agent (barter, change, exchange, swap, trade). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Gwen exchanged the dress for a shirt.".
Exclude: Agent acts upon Theme, Goal (omit, exclude, preclude). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They excluded me from the meeting.".
Exhale: Agent acts upon Theme (defecate, exhale, hemorrhage, perspire, expectorate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Paul exhaled.".
Flinch: Experiencer acts upon Stimulus (balk, cower, cringe, flinch, quail). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "Sharon flinched.".
Focus: Agent acts upon Topic (focus, focalize, center, concentrate, contemplate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "We focused on it.".
Forbid: Causer acts upon Eventuality, Beneficiary (keep, ban, bar, block, exclude). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "The rules forbade us from smoking.".
Free: Causer acts upon Source, Theme (alleviate, liberate, discharge, exonerate, unburden). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "It freed him of guilt.".
Function: Theme acts upon Attribute (go, go_on, run, perform, function). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "The old dishwasher still functioned.".
Help: Agent acts upon Beneficiary, Theme (support, succor, aid, abet, assist). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I helped him.".
Hold: Agent acts upon Theme (wield, handle, clasp, clutch, grab). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She held the rail.".
Involve: Agent acts upon Theme, Goal (involve, engage, relate, include, enroll). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They thoughtlessly involved me.".
Keep: Agent acts upon Theme, Location (hoard, hold, keep, leave, store). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Michelle kept the papers in the desk.".
Let: Agent acts upon Beneficiary, Eventuality (let). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They let us smoke.".
Limit: Causer acts upon Patient, Goal (confine, limit, reduce, restrict, restrain). The grammatical subject is the causer. Example: "The lack of money limited the help.".
Linger: Agent acts upon Location, Eventuality (dally, dawdle, dither, equivocate, hesitate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Sasha dawdled in the museum.".
Matter: Experiencer acts upon Stimulus (matter, count). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "It matters.".
Neglect: Agent acts upon Theme (disregard, forgo, forego, ignore, leave_out). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I disregarded the message.".
Orbit: Theme acts upon Axis (orbit, revolve, rotate, twirl). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "The Earth revolved around the Sun.".
Pain: Experiencer acts upon Patient, Stimulus (ail, bother, burn, hurt, itch). The grammatical subject is the experiencer. Example: "My eyes were itching.".
Patent: Agent acts upon Theme, Result (copyright, patent, trademark, license, register). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I patented my idea.".
Play: Agent acts upon Co-Agent, Theme (frolic, recreate, play). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Mary played.".
Promote: Agent acts upon Theme (promote, advance, boost, further, encourage). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "We promoted the cause.".
Prosecute: Agent acts upon Patient, Theme, Attribute (book, bust, collar, impeach, nab). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "They collared the suspect.".
Rebel: Agent acts upon Maleficiary, Theme (discriminate, rebel, retaliate, sin, legislate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "John rebelled.".
Refrain: Agent acts upon Eventuality (refrain, abstain, desist, forbear). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He refrained.".
Rely: Agent acts upon Theme (rely, depend, bank, bargain, count). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She relies on coming back in time.".
Resign: Agent acts upon Source, Attribute (leave_office, step_down, walk_out, resign, retire). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He resigned.".
Risk: Agent acts upon Pivot, Theme, Eventuality (bet, gamble, punt, venture, wager). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I risked my job.".
Rotate: Agent acts upon Theme, Extent, Trajectory (curl, coil, hook, rotate, spin). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The camera rotated 90 degrees.".
Rush: Agent acts upon Theme, Eventuality (hasten, hurry, rush). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Maggie hurried with her lunch.".
Seem: Theme acts upon Attribute (smell, sound, taste, keep, act). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "He appeared crazy.".
Shake: Agent acts upon Patient, Co-Patient (lump, mass, beat, fuse, scramble). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "The sticks bundled easily.".
Spend_Time: Agent acts upon Duration, Attribute (kill, lead, pass, serve, misspend). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I misspent 5 hours.".
Stop: Affector acts upon Eventuality, Subeventuality (cut, discontinue, kill, close, cut_off). The grammatical subject is the affector. Example: "John cut the engine.".
Subjugate: Agent acts upon Patient, Instrument (disenfranchise, enslave, mob, nourish, suppress). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Winnie the Pooh subjugated the unfortunate Pixies.".
Subordinate: Agent acts upon Co-Agent (answer, report). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Kevin reported to Martha.".
Succeed: Agent acts upon Eventuality (succeed, excel, manage, win, fall). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I succeeded.".
Suspect: Agent acts upon Theme, Attribute (suspect, accuse, condemn). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I suspected him of infidelity.".
Sustain: Agent acts upon Theme, Instrument, Attribute (hold, maintain, sustain, prolong, protract). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I maintained a strong government through ruthless domination.".
Terminus: Theme acts upon Location (begin, end, lead, start, stop). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "His property begins at the fence.".
Trifle: Agent acts upon Theme (dally, diddle, fiddle, play, toy). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "She trifled with him.".
Try: Agent acts upon Eventuality (try, attempt). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I tried apologizing.".
Void: Agent acts upon Patient (annul, quash, void, avoid, invalidate). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "I nullified their advantage.".
Volunteer: Agent acts upon Pivot, Theme, Attribute (offer, enlist, hire_on, sign_on, sign_up). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "John volunteered my sister for the task.".
Weekend: Theme acts upon Location (december, holiday, honeymoon, junket, overnight). The grammatical subject is the theme. Example: "My family always summered at the seashore.".
Withdraw: Agent acts upon Source (back_out, chicken_out, get_out, wriggle_out, bow_out). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "He withdrew.".
Work: Agent acts upon Co-Agent, Theme (bang_away, labor, labour, plug_away, slog_away). The grammatical subject is the agent. Example: "Amanda worked.".
